channel.title: EDGAR Live Scanner
channel.description: High-signal SEC EDGAR filings — tender offers, going-private, activist stakes, spinoffs, delistings, busted deals.

item.title: ⚔️ Proxy Contest · SEER
item.ns0:headline: Nominates 3 directors at Seer; pushes for $2.40/share sale
item.description:
Nominates 3 directors at Seer; pushes for $2.40/share sale

The Radoff-JEC Group, owning 7.8% of Seer, filed a preliminary proxy to elect three director nominees and reiterated its $2.40/share cash + CVR acquisition proposal. The group criticizes the board for rejecting its offers without engagement and calls for a strategic review. Annual meeting date not specified; proxy contest pending.

About: Seer, Inc., a life sciences company, engages in developing and commercializing products to decode the secrets of the proteome. It develops Proteograph Product Suite, an integrated solution that comprises consumables, an automation instrumentation, and software that allows researchers to conduct proteomic studies in therapeutic and diagnostic research, and clinical trials.

  • Offer price: $2.40 cash + CVR
  • Premium: 42%
  • Stake: 7.8%
  • Consideration: cash + CVR
  • Announced: 2026-04-13

item.title: ⚔️ Proxy Contest · GNK
item.ns0:headline: Increases tender offer to $24.80/sh cash; nominates 6 directors at GNK AGM June 18
item.description:
Increases tender offer to $24.80/sh cash; nominates 6 directors at GNK AGM June 18

Diana Shipping increased its all-cash tender offer for Genco Shipping & Trading to $24.80 per share from $23.50, a 39% premium to the undisturbed price. The offer expires June 26, 2026, and Diana is also soliciting proxies for its six independent director nominees at Genco's annual meeting on June 18, 2026. Diana has $1.433 billion in fully committed financing from a syndicate of banks.

About: Genco Shipping & Trading Limited, together with its subsidiaries, engages in the ocean transportation of dry bulk cargoes worldwide. The company owns and operates dry bulk carrier vessels to transports iron ore, coal, grains, steel products, and other dry-bulk cargoes. It charters its vessels primarily to trading houses, including commodities traders; producers; and government-owned entities.

  • Offer price: 24.80
  • Premium: 39%
  • Consideration: cash
  • Announced: 2026-05-27
  • Expected Close: June 26, 2026

Px $24.14 · Mkt cap $1.05B · EV $944.0M · Fwd P/E 10.9x · Fwd EV/EBITDA 7.8x · EV/Sales 2.6x · Industrials / Marine Shipping

item.title: 🤝 Merger · NHC
item.ns0:headline: Acquires NHI assets; $475M term loan + $50M revolver; close expected July 1
item.description:
Acquires NHI assets; $475M term loan + $50M revolver; close expected July 1

Corporation entered into a $475 million senior unsecured term loan and $50 million revolving credit facility to finance its previously announced acquisition of assets and real property from National Health Investors, Inc. (NHI). The transaction is expected to close in Q3 2026, currently anticipated on or around July 1, 2026, subject to expiration of the HSR waiting period. The new credit facility will replace the existing credit agreement, which had no outstanding balance as of March 31, 2026.

About: National HealthCare Corporation operates, manages, and provides services to skilled nursing facilities, assisted living facilities, independent living facilities, homecare and hospice agencies, and a behavioral health hospital. Its skilled nursing facilities offer licensed therapy services, nutrition services, social services, activities, and housekeeping and laundry services, as well as medical services prescribed by physicians; and rehabilitative services, such as physical, speech, respiratory…

  • Counterparty: National Health Investors, Inc. (NHI)
  • Expected Close: 2026-07-01

item.title: 📤 Divestiture · FREVS
item.ns0:headline: Sells Westwood Plaza shopping center to Regency Centers for $28.8M cash
item.ns0:shortName: FIRST REAL ESTATE INVESTMENT TRUST OF NEW JERSEY
item.description:
Sells Westwood Plaza shopping center to Regency Centers for $28.8M cash

Entered into a Purchase and Sale Agreement to sell its Westwood Plaza shopping center to an affiliate of Regency Centers Corporation for $28.8 million cash. The buyer deposited $1.2M refundable during a 120-day due diligence period ending September 23, 2026, after which it becomes non-refundable. Closing is expected by August 15, 2027, with no financing contingency.

About: First Real Estate Investment Trust of New Jersey is a publicly traded (over-the-counter  symbol FREVS.) REIT organized in 1961. Its portfolio of residential and commercial properties are located in New Jersey, New York and Maryland, with the largest concentration in northern New Jersey.

  • Counterparty: Regency Centers Corporation (affiliate)
  • Deal value: $28,800,000
  • Consideration: cash
  • Announced: 2026-05-26
  • Expected Close: by August 15, 2027

item.title: 🤝 Merger · SOUN
item.ns0:headline: Acquires LivePerson for ~$42.8M in stock; ~$3.33/share implied value
item.description:
Acquires LivePerson for ~$42.8M in stock; ~$3.33/share implied value

Is acquiring LivePerson, Inc. (LPSN) via a merger. LivePerson stockholders will receive a variable number of SOUN shares per LPSN share, with an implied value of approximately $3.33 per share (22% premium over 30-day VWAP). The aggregate consideration is $42.8M, subject to cash adjustments. The merger requires LivePerson stockholder approval; a special meeting will be held on a date to be set. The SOUN stock price used in the exchange ratio is collared between $7.00 and $12.00 per share.

About: SoundHound AI, Inc. develops independent voice artificial intelligence (AI) platform that enables businesses across industries to deliver high-quality conversational experiences to their customers. Its products include Houndify platform that offers a suite of Houndify tools to help brands build conversational voice assistants, such as automatic speech recognition, natural language understanding, wake words, custom domains, text-to-speech, and embedded voice solutions The company is headquartered…

  • Counterparty: LivePerson, Inc.
  • Offer price: ~$3.33 per LPSN share (implied)
  • Deal value: $42,784,532.64
  • Premium: 22%
  • Consideration: stock
  • Announced: 2026-04-21
  • Expected Close: after special meeting (date TBD)

item.title: 🤝 Merger · NDTAF
item.ns0:headline: Acquires Northern Data for 2.0281 RUM shares per share; tender expires June 1
item.description:
Acquires Northern Data for 2.0281 RUM shares per share; tender expires June 1

Rumble Inc.'s exchange offer to acquire all outstanding shares of Northern Data AG expires June 1, 2026, with closing expected mid-June. Each Northern Data share receives 2.0281 newly issued Rumble Class A common shares. All regulatory approvals have been received, and the offer is not conditioned on a minimum tender threshold.

About: Northern Data AG develops, builds, and operates high-performance computing (HPC) infrastructure solutions in Germany and internationally. It offers infrastructure for various HPC applications in the areas of cloud computing, cryptocurrency hosting services and hardware, and stationary and mobile data centers. The company was formerly known as Northern Bitcoin AG and changed its name to Northern Data AG in January 2020. Northern Data AG is headquartered in Frankfurt am Main, Germany.

  • Offer price: 2.0281 RUM shares per NDTAF share
  • Consideration: stock
  • Expected Close: mid-June 2026

item.title: 🤝 Merger · FABP
item.ns0:headline: Acquires Farmers Bancorp for 3.40 RMBI shares per share; vote passed
item.description:
Acquires Farmers Bancorp for 3.40 RMBI shares per share; vote passed

Richmond Mutual Bancorporation stockholders approved the merger with The Farmers Bancorp. Farmers Bancorp shareholders will receive 3.40 shares of RMBI common stock for each Farmers share in a tax-free exchange. The transaction is expected to close around the end of Q2 2026, and the combined company will continue to trade as RMBI.

About: Farmers Bancorp (Frankfort IN) operates as the holding company of The Farmers Bank that provides a range of banking products and services in the United States. The company offers deposit products; and commercial and agricultural, commercial real estate, construction, and residential and consumer loans. The company was founded in 1876 and is based in Frankfort, Indiana.

  • Counterparty: The Farmers Bancorp
  • Offer price: 3.40 RMBI shares
  • Consideration: stock
  • Announced: 2025-11-11
  • Expected Close: Q2 2026

item.title: ⚔️ Proxy Contest · PCRX
item.ns0:headline: Faces DOMA Perpetual proxy contest; AGM June 9
item.description:
Faces DOMA Perpetual proxy contest; AGM June 9

Faces a proxy contest from DOMA Perpetual Capital Management, which has nominated three director candidates (Christopher Dennis, Oliver Benton Curtis III, Eric de Armas) for election at the June 9, 2026 annual meeting. Pacira urges stockholders to vote for its own nominees (Christopher Christie, Samit Hirawat, Thomas Wiggans), citing strong performance including record 2025 revenues of $726.4M, GAAP net income of $7.0M, and a favorable EXPAREL settlement providing exclusivity into 2030. The company's board has met with DOMA 17 times since 2023 and interviewed two of its nominees.

About: Pacira BioSciences, Inc. provides non-opioid pain management and regenerative health solutions for healthcare practitioners and their patients in the United States. The company offers EXPAREL, a bupivacaine liposome injectable suspension; ZILRETTA, a triamcinolone acetonide extended-release injectable suspension; and iovera system, a non-opioid handheld cryoanalgesia device used to produce controlled doses of cold temperature only to targeted nerves.

  • Counterparty: DOMA Perpetual Capital Management

item.title: 👀 Activist · LULU
item.ns0:headline: Reaches cooperation agreement with Chip Wilson; adds 2 directors, declassifies board
item.description:
Reaches cooperation agreement with Chip Wilson; adds 2 directors, declassifies board

lululemon entered into a cooperation agreement with founder Chip Wilson, who owns approximately 8.7% of the company. The board will appoint Laura Gentile and Marc Maurer as independent directors following the 2026 annual meeting, declassify the board, and add a third new director by October 1, 2026. Wilson agreed to standstill, voting commitments, and non-disparagement for about 18 months.

About: lululemon athletica inc., together with its subsidiaries, designs, distributes, and retails athletic apparel and accessories for women and men. It operates in two segments, Company-Operated Stores and Direct to Consumer. The company offers pants, shorts, tops, and jackets for healthy lifestyle and athletic activities, such as yoga, running, and training, as well as other sweaty pursuits. It also provides fitness-related accessories and footwear.

  • Counterparty: Chip Wilson
  • Stake: 8.7%
  • Announced: 2026-05-26

Px $126.76 · Mkt cap $15.15B · EV $21.30B · Fwd P/E 10.3x · Fwd EV/EBITDA 7.4x · EV/Sales 1.9x · Consumer Cyclical / Apparel - Retail

item.title: 👀 Activist · LULU
item.ns0:headline: Settles proxy contest with Chip Wilson; appoints 2 directors, backs declassification
item.description:
Settles proxy contest with Chip Wilson; appoints 2 directors, backs declassification

Entered into a cooperation agreement with founder Chip Wilson, resolving his proxy contest. The board will appoint Laura Gentile and Marc Maurer as independent directors after the 2026 annual meeting (targeted June 25, 2026), and a third new director with apparel expertise by October 1, 2026. The company will recommend a vote in favor of Wilson's declassification proposal at the annual meeting; Wilson withdraws his director nominations and books-and-records demand and agrees to a standstill and voting commitments.

About: lululemon athletica inc., together with its subsidiaries, designs, distributes, and retails athletic apparel and accessories for women and men. It operates in two segments, Company-Operated Stores and Direct to Consumer. The company offers pants, shorts, tops, and jackets for healthy lifestyle and athletic activities, such as yoga, running, and training, as well as other sweaty pursuits. It also provides fitness-related accessories and footwear.

  • Counterparty: Chip Wilson (Anamered Investments Inc. et al.)
  • Announced: 2026-05-26
  • Expected Close: 2026-06-25

item.title: 👀 Activist · Cooperation Agreement · SNPS
item.ns0:headline: Settles with Elliott; Cohn joins board, standstill at 4.9% ownership
item.description:
Settles with Elliott; Cohn joins board, standstill at 4.9% ownership

Entered a cooperation agreement with Elliott Investment Management L.P. Elliott's Jesse Cohn will join the board as an independent director effective June 1, 2026. The agreement includes voting commitments, standstill restrictions limiting Elliott to 4.9% beneficial ownership and 7.5% aggregate economic exposure, and mutual non-disparagement provisions. The cooperation period lasts until one year after the agreement or 10 days after Cohn leaves the board.

About: Synopsys, Inc. provides electronic design automation software products used to design and test integrated circuits. The company offers Fusion Design Platform that provides digital design implementation solutions; Verification Continuum Platform that provides virtual prototyping, static and formal verification, simulation, emulation, field programmable gate array (FPGA)-based prototyping, and debug solutions; and FPGA design products that are programmed to perform specific functions.

  • Counterparty: Elliott Investment Management L.P.
  • Announced: 2026-05-26

item.title: 💧 Capital Return · Buyback Authorization · E
item.ns0:headline: Launches second tranche of €2.8B buyback program; up to 9.8% of shares
item.description:
Launches second tranche of €2.8B buyback program; up to 9.8% of shares

Eni S.p.A. announced the termination of the first tranche and the launch of the second tranche of its €2.8 billion treasury shares buyback program. The second tranche will concern up to 297.9 million shares, representing approximately 9.8% of the share capital, and has a maximum duration until the end of April 2027. Shares acquired in this tranche will be cancelled to provide additional shareholder remuneration.

About: Eni S.p.A. engages in the exploration, development, and production of crude oil and natural gas. It operates through Exploration & Production; Global Gas & LNG Portfolio; Refining & Marketing and Chemicals; Plenitude and Power; and Corporate and Other activities segments. The Exploration & Production segment is involved in the research, development, and production of oil, condensates and natural gas; and forestry conservation and CO2 capture and storage projects.

  • Deal value: 2800000000

item.description:

Remains under court-appointed receivership. The receiver's Seventh Interim Report covers activities through April 21, 2026, including the resignation of the independent auditor, status of audits for FY2023 and FY2024, accounting of misappropriated funds, real property and storage matters, and pending litigation. The receivership is ongoing with no resolution yet.

About: Global Tech Industries Group, Inc. operates an online cryptocurrency trading platform in the United States. It operates Beyond Blockchain, a cryptocurrency trading platform, which allows multi-currency clearing and direct settlements in Bitcoin, Ethereum, Tether, Bitcoin Cash, Litecoin, Bitcoin SV, Aave, Compound, Uniswap, Chainlink, and Yearn Finance. The company was formerly known as Tree Top Industries, Inc. and changed its name to Global Tech Industries Group, Inc. in July 2016.

item.description:
Acquires Passport for $350M (half cash, half stock); close July 2026

Agreed to acquire Passport Global Inc., a US-based cross-border e-commerce logistics company, for $350 million in upfront consideration funded by approximately equal portions of cash and ordinary shares, plus up to $75 million contingent on Passport achieving certain 2026 financial results. Passport is expected to generate ~$100M revenue in 2026; the transaction is expected to close in early July 2026, subject to customary closing conditions and regulatory approvals. Passport's founder and CEO Alex Yancher will join Global-e's executive management team to lead the integrated business.

About: Global-E Online Ltd., together with its subsidiaries, provides a platform to enable and accelerate direct-to-consumer cross-border e-commerce in Israel, the United Kingdom, the United States, and internationally.   • Expected Close: early July 2026

item.description:

Announced that the European Commission granted marketing authorization for Joenja (leniolisib) for the treatment of activated PI3Kδ syndrome (APDS) in patients aged 12 and older. This is the first approved treatment for APDS in the European Union, with the first launch expected in Germany in Q3 2026. The approval is based on a Phase II/III trial showing statistically significant improvements in immune dysregulation markers.

item.ns0:headline: Calls special meeting to elect board majority; urges dual-track sale/lease process
item.description:
Calls special meeting to elect board majority; urges dual-track sale/lease process

Toby Neugebauer, co-founder and largest shareholder of Fermi Inc., delivered a shareholder presentation calling for a special meeting to elect a new board majority. He argues the board must run a dual-track process to explore a sale or lease of the company's Project Matador assets, citing risks of dilution, financing, and counterparty exposure. Neugebauer states he is not seeking the CEO role and wants an independent committee to maximize risk-adjusted value for all shareholders.

About: Fermi, Inc. engages in the development of energy infrastructure. It intends to develop an energy and data center development campus to support the needs of to-be-built AI infrastructure. The company was founded by Rick Perry, Toby Neugebauer and Griffin Perry on January 10, 2025 and is headquartered in Amrillo, TX.

  • Announced: 2026-05-21

item.title: 📤 Divestiture · SKIL
item.ns0:headline: Sells Global Knowledge Training to EHJob GP for up to $18M; close Q2
item.description:
Sells Global Knowledge Training to EHJob GP for up to $18M; close Q2

Entered into a definitive agreement to sell its Global Knowledge Training business (instructor-led training) to EHJob GP LLC for total consideration of up to $18 million, consisting of $10 million initial cash at closing (subject to working capital adjustments) plus $10 million deferred consideration less approximately $2 million for long-term employee liabilities, payable in five quarterly installments. The initial payment may be funded by a seller note from the business to Skillsoft. Skillsoft intends to use net proceeds for general corporate purposes and to focus on its core AI-native skills management platform. The transaction is subject to customary closing conditions, including Saudi Arabia competition clearance, and is expected to close in the second fiscal quarter (by July 31, 2026).

About: Skillsoft Corp. provides corporate digital learning services in the United States and internationally. The company operates through Content, Global Knowledge, and SumTotal segments. It offers enterprise learning solutions to prepare organizations for the future of work, as well as enable them to overcome critical skill gaps, drive demonstrable behavior-change, and unlock the potential in their greatest assets.

  • Counterparty: EHJob GP LLC
  • Deal value: up to $18,000,000
  • Consideration: cash
  • Announced: 2026-05-20
  • Expected Close: Q2 FY2027 (by July 31, 2026)

Ford closed the disposition of its BlueOval SK joint venture interest, with subsidiary Ford Energy Battery acquiring the two Kentucky battery plants and assuming a $3.805B DOE loan at 4.814% interest. Ford's capital contribution obligation of up to $6.6B was terminated, and it was released from its guarantee under the SSA. The loan requires quarterly interest-only payments through Jan 2030, then principal and interest through July 2040.

item.title: ⚖️ Litigation · Won · HCM
item.ns0:headline: Won NMPA approval for fruquintinib/sintilimab combo in RCC
item.description:
Won NMPA approval for fruquintinib/sintilimab combo in RCC

HUTCHMED and Innovent announced NMPA approval for ELUNATE (fruquintinib) plus TYVYT (sintilimab) for second-line advanced/metastatic renal cell carcinoma. The FRUSICA-2 study showed a 63% reduction in risk of progression or death (HR 0.373) and median PFS of 22.2 months vs 6.9 months for axitinib/everolimus. This is the 10th approved indication for sintilimab.

About: HUTCHMED (China) Limited discovers, develops, and commercializes targeted therapeutics and immunotherapies for cancer and immunological diseases in HongKong and internationally. It operates in Oncology/Immunology and Other Ventures segments. The company develops Savolitinib, an inhibitor for non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC), papillary and renal cell carcinoma, colorectal cancer (CRC), and gastric cancer (GC); and Fruquintinib, an inhibitor for CRC, breast cancer, GC, endometrial cancer (EMC), …

item.description:
Nominates 3 directors at MFIN; ISS governance deck filed; AGM June 9

BIMIZCI Fund LLC, together with Warnke Investments, ZimCal Asset Management, and Stephen Hodges, filed an ISS governance presentation highlighting governance flaws and nominating three independent director candidates (Eric Kelly, John Kiernan, Timothy Shanahan) for election at Medallion Financial's June 9, 2026 annual meeting. BIMIZCI holds 500,205 shares (4th largest institutional holder) and $15M par value of trust preferred securities. The presentation details a family-led board, poor compensation practices, and a six-pillar value creation plan.

About: Medallion Financial Corp., together with its subsidiaries, operates as a finance company in the United States. The company operates through four segments: Recreation Lending, Home Improvement Lending, Commercial Lending, and Medallion Lending. It provides loans that finance consumer purchases of recreational vehicles, boats, and trailers; consumer home improvements; commercial businesses; and taxi medallions to individuals, and small to mid-size businesses.

item.description:

The special meeting is scheduled for June 26, 2026; the board and a special committee unanimously recommend approval.

About: Select Medical Holdings Corporation, through its subsidiaries, operates critical illness recovery hospitals, rehabilitation hospitals, outpatient rehabilitation clinics, and occupational health centers in the United States. Completion is subject to regulatory approvals, which are expected to require Prudential to reduce its shareholding in ICICI Prudential Life Insurance to under 10%. About: Prudential plc, through its subsidiaries, provides life and health insurance, and retirement and asset management solutions to individuals in Asia, and Africa. item.ns0:headline: Acquires Orphai in stock merger; pre-deal holders retain ~17.8% stake
item.description:
Acquires Orphai in stock merger; pre-deal holders retain ~17.8% stake

Acquired Orphai Therapeutics in a stock-for-stock merger, issuing 3,258,517 common shares and 67,101.235 Series C convertible preferred shares (each convertible into 1,000 common shares). Pre-transaction Quince shareholders hold approximately 17.8% of the combined company on a fully-diluted basis (6.9% after a concurrent financing), while former Orphai holders hold 82.2% (31.9% after financing). The board unanimously approved; a stockholders' meeting will be held to approve conversion of preferred stock and a charter amendment to increase authorized shares to 800 million.

About: Quince Therapeutics, Inc., a biopharmaceutical company, focuses on advancing precision therapeutics for debilitating and rare diseases. The company has discovered a broad bone-targeting drug platform to precisely deliver small molecules, peptides, or large molecules directly to the site of bone fracture and disease. Its lead compound is NOV004, an anabolic peptide engineered to precisely target and concentrate at the bone fracture site The company was formerly known as Cortexyme, Inc.

Neurocrine Biosciences completed its acquisition of Soleno Therapeutics via a cash tender offer at $53.00 per share, representing a total equity value of $2.9 billion. As of the May 15 expiration, 46,356,114 shares (88.9% of outstanding) were validly tendered; all conditions were satisfied, and the merger was effected under Section 251(h) of the DGCL on May 18, 2026. Soleno's common stock will be delisted from Nasdaq.

About: Soleno Therapeutics, Inc., a clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company, focuses on the development and commercialization of novel therapeutics for the treatment of rare diseases. Its lead candidate is Diazoxide Choline Controlled-Release, a once-daily oral tablet for the treatment of Prader-Willi Syndrome, which is being evaluated in an ongoing Phase III clinical development program. The company was formerly known as Capnia, Inc. and changed its name to Soleno Therapeutics, Inc. in May 2017.

item.title: 🤝 Merger · D
item.ns0:headline: Acquires Dominion Energy for $360M cash + 0.8138 NEE shares/share; votes pending
item.description:
Acquires Dominion Energy for $360M cash + 0.8138 NEE shares/share; votes pending

NextEra Energy (NEE) will acquire Dominion Energy (D) in a cash-and-stock transaction. Each Dominion share will be converted into a pro rata share of $360 million aggregate cash plus 0.8138 NextEra shares. The merger requires approval from both NextEra and Dominion shareholders, as well as regulatory clearances from FERC, NRC, Virginia SCC, North Carolina UC, and South Carolina PSC. The combined company will maintain Dominion's headquarters in Richmond, VA and an operating headquarters in Cayce, SC.

About: Dominion Energy, Inc. produces and distributes energy in the United States. The company operates through four segments: Dominion Energy Virginia, Gas Distribution, Dominion Energy South Carolina, and Contracted Assets. The Dominion Energy Virginia segment generates, transmits, and distributes regulated electricity to approximately 2.7 million residential, commercial, industrial, and governmental customers in Virginia and North Carolina.

  • Counterparty: NextEra Energy
  • Consideration: cash+stock
  • Announced: 2026-05-15

item.title: ⚖️ Litigation · Won · GPRO
item.ns0:headline: Wins patent case; $8.2M award vacated; all claims invalid
item.description:
Wins patent case; $8.2M award vacated; all claims invalid

The U.S. District Court for the Northern District of California granted GoPro's motion for judgment as a matter of law, invalidating the sole remaining patent claim (claim 11 of U.S. Patent No. 8,890,954) as obvious. The court vacated the $8.2 million damages award from the October 2025 jury verdict and denied all of Contour IP Holding's post-trial motions. GoPro has no liability from the case, though the ruling is subject to appeal to the Federal Circuit.

About: GoPro, Inc. engages in manufacturing and selling cameras and camera accessories. It provides mountable and wearable cameras and accessories, which it refers to as capture devices. Its product brands include HERO9 Black, HERO8 Black, Max, HERO7 Black, HERO7 Silver, GoPro Plus, and GoPro App. The company was founded by Nicholas Woodman in 2002 and is headquartered in San Mateo, CA.

  • Counterparty: Contour IP Holding LLC
  • Announced: 2026-05-14

item.title: 🤝 Merger · SACH
item.ns0:headline: IRG contributes $2.9B portfolio for 94.1% stake; $2.00/share implied value; vote pending
item.description:
IRG contributes $2.9B portfolio for 94.1% stake; $2.00/share implied value; vote pending

Entered into a Contribution Agreement with Industrial Realty Group Global to contribute IRG's industrial real estate portfolio (98 assets, ~$2.9B gross asset value) in exchange for ~94.1% of the combined company's OP units and Class B shares with 51% voting power. Sachem existing shareholders will retain ~5.9% ownership; the transaction implies a $2.00 per share value for Sachem common stock, a 90% premium to the 30-day VWAP. The deal is subject to Sachem shareholder approval, regulatory conditions, and pre-closing reorganization including a 20-to-1 reverse stock split, redomestication to Delaware, and name change to IRG Realty Trust; closing expected by end of 2026.

About: Sachem Capital Corp. operates as a real estate finance company. The company is involved in the originating, underwriting, funding, servicing, and managing a portfolio of short-term loans secured by first mortgage liens on real property located primarily in Northeastern United States and Florida. It offers loans to real estate investors and owners to fund their acquisition, renovation, rehabilitation, development, and/or improvement of residential or commercial properties.

  • Counterparty: Industrial Realty Group Global
  • Offer price: $2.00 per share (implied)
  • Deal value: ~$3.4B implied combined enterprise value
  • Premium: 90%
  • Consideration: stock
  • Announced: 2026-05-17
  • Expected Close: end of 2026

item.description:

Its flagship asset is the Farellón project that consists of 8 mining concessions covering an area of approximately 1,234 hectares located in the Carrizal Alto mining district, Chile.

  • Announced: 2026-05-13
  • Expected Close: 90 days after filing

item.title: 🤝 Merger · BSTR Holdings
item.ns0:headline: SPAC CEPO merges with BSTR Bitcoin business; $400M PIPE, $574.7M notes
item.description:
SPAC CEPO merges with BSTR Bitcoin business; $400M PIPE, $574.7M notes

Cantor Equity Partners I (CEPO), a SPAC, will merge with BSTR Newco, a Bitcoin-focused business, with the combined entity to be publicly traded as BSTR Holdings. CEPO shareholders will receive shares of Pubco Class A common stock. The transaction includes a $400M cash PIPE, $574.7M convertible notes, and $256.6M preferred stock private placements. The Seller receives Pubco shares based on the Bitcoin price. A shareholder vote is pending; closing is subject to approval and other conditions.

  • Counterparty: Cantor Equity Partners I (SPAC)

  • Announced: 2025-07-16

item.description:

Key milestones include a deSPAC vote in Q2, limited notice to proceed in Q3, and first power purchase agreement in Q4 2026.

item.ns0:headline: Oppose CCM merger; UWMC offers $12.50/sh cash or 2.3328 UWMC shares; vote May 19
item.description:
Oppose CCM merger; UWMC offers $12.50/sh cash or 2.3328 UWMC shares; vote May 19

UWM Holdings (UWMC) is soliciting proxies for Two Harbors (TWO) stockholders to vote AGAINST the proposed merger with CrossCountry (CCM) at $12.00/share. UWMC has made a superior proposal offering $12.50 cash or 2.3328 UWMC shares per TWO share, with no cap or proration. The special meeting is May 19, 2026; ISS and Glass Lewis recommend voting against the CCM merger.

About: Two Harbors Investment Corp. operates as a real estate investment trust (REIT) that focuses on investing in, financing, and managing residential mortgage-backed securities (RMBS), non-agency securities, mortgage servicing rights, and other financial assets in the United States. Its target assets include agency RMBS collateralized by fixed rate mortgage loans, adjustable rate mortgage loans, and hybrid adjustable-rate mortgage (ARMs); and other assets, such as financial and mortgage-related asset…

item.description:
